essence: David asks how the Portable Brain scale work should run as a background task, including how many parallel sub-brains to pick and which ones.
David confirms question two of the library spec is library-only scope and asks for clearer explanation of question three and of option A vs option B in question one.

Spec A (prerequisite work) is done and David directs the session to write a handover document plus completion notes before merging, so Spec B — the actual database column upgrade — can be written in a fresh conversation, with open questions on an unexpected migration, the test-ID hardening story, NPM publish, 2FA bypass token automation, and a missing Linear ticket.

essence: David asks whether pushing closes the current feature branch and resets memory to main, and whether the Scale work should run in a new branch given only a couple of documents appear to change apart from the dot-probes work.

Before closing the session David decides that saving audit data and documenting current metrics is the smart move — accumulating problem understanding on each run even without code changes — while deferring the choice between committing milestone C, scaling, or going deeper.
